text: Background Objective To determine total phenolic and flavonoid content, color and antioxidant activity of honey samples obtained from ten colonies of Apis mellifera L. in Roraima State, Brazil. Methods Honey samples from beehives were submitted to spectrophotometry assays to determine their color and, by using Folin-Ciocalteu reagent, total phenolic content. In addition, using aluminum chloride (spectrophotometry with the method of total flavones/flavonols) and 2,4-dinitrophenylhydrazine (spectrophotometry with the method of flavonones/dihydroflavonols), the total flavonoid content was also calculated and identified. The 2,2-diphenyl-1-picrylhydrazyl (DPPH) assay was used as a radical scavenger to verify antioxidant activity. The inhibitory concentration 50 (IC50) values were calculated. Results and discussion Quantities of total phenolic content varied between 250 and 548 mg gallic acid kg-1 of honey; whereas amounts of total flavonoids ranged from 9 to 48.6 mg of quercetine kg-1 of honey, and from 1805 to 2606 mg of pinocembrin kg-1 of honey. The values of IC50 of honey samples from colonies of Apis mellifera L. were between 3.17 and 8.79 mg/mL. Pearson correlations showed that, the higher the color intensity, the higher the contents of flavones, flavonols and phenolics. On the other hand, the higher the antioxidant capacity the lower the amount of constituents.
